## Zakres zmian (cała gałąź)
### 1. Academy / adapter runtime (backend)

title: [PR] feat: Engine Resilience v8.0 - Autonomous Loop Breaking & Qualitative Insights
summary:
### Overview 🌟
This PR significantly hardens the Specter testing engine against repetitive stalled states and introduces high-value qualitative data for the end-user. We've moved from reactive loop breaking to proactive "Depth-First" cognitive processing.

### Key Changes ✨
- **Cycle Detection**

title: [PR] fix(config): warn when configured model primary is not in catalog
summary:
## Summary

When a user sets `agents.defaults.model.primary` or `agents.list[].model` to a model name that does not exist in the runtime catalog (e.g. `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-codex` before the Codex provider ships it), the config is accepted silently. Every cron job and heartbeat then fails at runtime

title: [PR] feat(groq): Add TTS adapter
summary:
Adds a tree-shakeable Text-to-Speech (TTS) adapter for the Groq API. This includes support for English and Arabic voices, various output formats (with WAV as default),
and configuration options like speed and sample rate.

Includes new types, model metadata for TTS models, and comprehensive unit

title: [PR] refactor: Convert Tanzu provider to declarative JSON config
summary:
## Summary

Replaces the dedicated ~1,000-line Rust Tanzu provider with a 21-line declarative JSON config, per feedback from @DOsinga and @katzdave. Adds generic `env_vars` and `dynamic_models` support to the declarative provider framework so all providers benefit.

This addresses all the review fee

title: [PR] UN-3104 feat: Add Unstract Adapter Extension Skill and Azure AI Foundry Adapter
summary:

- Add new Claude Code skill for automating adapter development workflows (`adapter-ops`)
- Add Azure AI Foundry LLM adapter with full configuration schema
- Update existing adapter JSON schemas (OpenAI, Azure, Bedrock, Mistral, Ollama) with improved parameter specifications

## Why

title: Custom OpenAI-compatible provider options not being passed to API calls

When using a custom provider with `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible`, the `options` (including `baseURL` and `apiKey`) configured in `opencode.json` are not being passed to the actual API calls. This res

title: [PR] fix: harden security, error handling, and parsing across wiki-generator

- Move Gemini API key from URL query param to x-goog-api-key header

- Add HTTPError handling to _call_ollama matching sibling methods
- Add exception chaining

title: [PR] Add lightweight Ollama adapter to Option B AI endpoint
summary:

- Connect the existing Option B AI REST endpoint to a configurable LLM provider (defaulting to Ollama) while keeping provider logic isolated from business/data code and avoiding UI changes.
- Provide strict JSON-oriented prompts/responses so existing actions can send structured inputs
